evals.prompt: Create a new API endpoint for managing blog posts. Each post has a title, body, author_id, and published boolean.
evals.expected_output: Complete CRUD endpoint with schema, service, router, and test files following project patterns
expectations: Creates a Pydantic schema file at services/backend/app/schemas/ with Create, Update, Response, and ListResponse models
Service class uses async SQLAlchemy 2.0 style (select().where()) not legacy query()
Service raises NotFoundError from core.exceptions, not HTTPException
Router uses Annotated dependency injection pattern (ServiceDep, DatabaseDep, UserDep)
Delete endpoint performs soft delete (is_deleted = True), not hard delete
Test file exists at services/backend/app/tests/ with at least create and not-found test cases
All DB operations are async (await db.execute, await db.commit)

evals.prompt: Add an endpoint for organization invitations. Fields: email, role (admin/member), org_id. Need to check for duplicate invitations.
evals.expected_output: CRUD endpoint with conflict detection for duplicate email+org combinations
expectations: Service includes duplicate check logic that raises ConflictError
Uses get_settings from core.config, not os.getenv
Schema uses Field validators for email format
List endpoint supports pagination with limit/offset query parameters
Router prefix and tags follow kebab-case convention

evals.prompt: I need a simple health-check style endpoint that returns the count of active projects. No auth needed, just GET /projects/stats
evals.expected_output: A read-only endpoint with service layer, not logic in the router
expectations: Business logic lives in a service class, not directly in the router function
Filters by is_deleted == False in the count query
Response uses a Pydantic schema, not a raw dict
